message.content: Fix the failing test in auth.test.ts

content.text: Let me check the test output first.

content.content:
FAIL auth.test.ts
  auth middleware
    ✗ should validate JWT token
      TypeError: Cannot read property 'verify' of undefined
        at auth.test.ts:15:22
    ✗ should reject expired token
      TypeError: Cannot read property 'verify' of undefined
        at auth.test.ts:28:22
    ✓ should pass through public routes

  1 passed, 2 failed

content.text: The mock for jwt.verify is not set up correctly. Let me update the mock.

input.new_string: const mockJwt = { sign: stub(), verify: stub() };

content.content:
FAIL auth.test.ts
  auth middleware
    ✗ should validate JWT token
      TypeError: Cannot read property 'verify' of undefined
        at auth.test.ts:15:22
    ✗ should reject expired token
      TypeError: Cannot read property 'verify' of undefined
        at auth.test.ts:28:22
    ✓ should pass through public routes

  1 passed, 2 failed

content.text: Same error. The mock is defined but module caching means the import already resolved before the mock was set up. I need to restructure the test to set up mocks before imports.

input.old_string:
import { authMiddleware } from './auth.middleware.ts';
const mockJwt = { sign: stub(), verify: stub() };

content.content:
ok | 3 passed | 0 failed (auth.test.ts)

  auth middleware
    ✓ should validate JWT token
    ✓ should reject expired token
    ✓ should pass through public routes

content.text: All tests pass now. The root cause was mock hoisting — mocks must be set up before the module under test is imported, otherwise the module caches the original (unmocked) implementation.
